owned_files.notes: 33 .rs files total. 27 user-facing page modules + mod.rs (37 LOC), placeholder.rs (32 LOC, 'coming soon' template), hud.rs (179 LOC), escape_menu.rs (263 LOC), main_menu.rs (366 LOC), ai_usage (79, doc-first stub)
Largest page — full server/DM/group view; 26 raw egui::Button calls (top migration target)
Thin — describes scope + tabs but no live proposal feed yet (web side same)
Documentation-first stub — points user at web page for the form
Read-only explainer; guardian flow lives elsewhere
Read-only DID viewer per file header

gaps: Parity gap vs web: native is missing dashboard, data, dev, download, home, index, ops, projects, roadmap, wallet-guide, web, admin (12 web-only pages)

Stub-tier pages (under ~200 LOC, mostly documentation): governance (80), ai_usage (79), recovery (106), identity (150), agents (138), placeholder (32) — these are real but thin compared to chat (3018) / settings (1314) / studio (749)
Active TODOs in chat.rs: 7 (DM mute toggle, group mute, sort, group voice routing, add-server modal, per-group admin role, mute action)
main_menu.rs:133 TODO — WebSocket connect not actually wired
Migration debt: 49 raw egui::Button usages across 11 page files (see widgets scope)
summary: 27 native pages registered, 13529 LOC. Strong on chat/settings/studio/maps; thin on governance/ai_usage/recovery/identity (treated as read-only viewers per design). Web has 12 pages with no native counterpart — the biggest parity gaps are projects, dashboard, ops, and admin.
